title: Unified stack registry + first-available-N allocation: one shared N-pool across dev+demo (stack-core/stack_registry.py) + the first-available-N allocator (flock'd, docker-ps reconcile, atomic write) + both up-paths wired (explicit-N or auto, teardown frees the slot) + the corpus/ops/rosetta_demo.md delivery
kind: the v1.3 foundation milestone — a NEW Python module (stack_registry.py) + shell-CLI wiring in dev-stack + demo-stack; no Go touched (M12 is the registry/port layer, not the snapshot/seeding Go modules). Tests are pytest + shellcheck.

tests.go_note: M12 touched ZERO Go — the unified registry is Python (stack_registry.py) + bash CLI wiring. The 708 Go total (alignment 46 / clerkenstein 218 / stack-seeding 232 / stack-snapshot 212) is carried unchanged from v1.2.
tests.python_delta: test_stack_registry.py 0 -> 50 NEW (28 at build, 49 after 3 harden passes, +1 at close); dev-stack/tests +1 (21 -> 22, the real ERR-trap-frees path); demo-stack/tests +1 (12 -> 13, same trap path). +52 net new Python test funcs across M12.
tests.close_added_test: test_concurrent_explicit_N_collision_exactly_one_wins (stack-core) — the Phase 2c adversarial regression: 6 OS processes claim --n 5, exactly 1 wins / 5 exit-2.
tests.race: n/a (no Go in M12); the cross-process race is exercised by 12 OS-process contention on the real fcntl.flock (test_cross_process_allocations_are_all_distinct) + the new explicit-N collision test
tests.flake_gate: 5/5 clean — sequential runs of all 3 M12-touched pytest suites (89 passed each run; stdlib unittest = deterministic intra-suite order, the realistic CI condition; pytest-randomly not installed)

coverage.note: harden ran 3 passes (recorded in progress.md). stack_registry.py statement coverage 92% -> 98% (2 residual misses are structurally unreachable: the main() argparse-guaranteed fallthrough + the __main__ guard). The bash CLIs are I/O-bound (offline-uncoverable) — guarded by shellcheck + the allocation contract per the milestone's test policy.
coverage.stack_registry_py: 98% statements (2 miss: lines 313, 317 — both structurally unreachable)

delivered.unified_registry: stack-core/stack_registry.py — one record per live stack keyed by docker project '<type>-<N>', {type,n,ports,status,created}, one shared N-pool across dev+demo (dev-1 and demo-1 can never coexist). Runtime file stack-core/.stacks/registry.json (gitignored), flock-guarded, atomic temp+os.replace writes, corrupt-JSON recovery (M12-D1).
delivered.first_available_n: allocate(type, n=None) — n=None auto-allocates the lowest free N across BOTH types; n=<int> validates it's free. Whole read-reconcile-pick-write under one fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX) on a sidecar .lock (portable macOS+Linux). Registry-of-record + docker-ps reconcile that only ADDS used-N (adopts unmanaged live stacks), never subtracts — release() is the only free path (M12-D2, M12-D3).
delivered.cli_wiring: dev-stack/dev-stack + demo-stack/rosetta-demo both: up [N] (explicit or 'auto'/omit), ERR-trap frees the slot on a failed bring-up (installed BEFORE the post-allocation re-guard), set-ports records resolved host ports, down releases. status leads with the unified dev+demo view (rosetta-demo also keeps the per-demo provenance registry).
delivered.corpus_delivery: corpus/ops/rosetta_demo.md — new 'Unified stack registry + first-available-N allocation (v1.3 M12)' section: the collision problem, the unified record, the allocator + adds-only reconcile + race guard, the dev,demo,dev,demo,demo -> dev-1,demo-2,dev-3,demo-4,demo-5 guarantee, pointers to stack-core/README.md + both CLI guides + the M14 forward-ref.
delivered.handbook: stack-core/README.md gained the stack_registry.py row + the full registry/allocator model; indexed in README.md + knowledge/README.md with M12 provenance. KB-1 resolved (demo-stack/GUIDE.md 'registry assigns N' rewritten to the true unified-allocator prose).

notes: M12 is the first milestone of v1.3 'stack party' and the only NON-Go milestone surface to date — the unified registry is Python + bash. The implementation held under every harden + adversarial probe (0 bugs across 3 harden passes + 4 close scenarios). The headline race guarantee (no two concurrent up's pick the same N) is verified at the OS-process level (12-process auto-allocation + 6-process explicit-N collision), not just threads. Next: M13 (dev peers).
